name: Rich brief but no design system data — tests graceful degradation

input.userMessage: Build a team members page. Shows a table of all team members with name, email, role (Admin/Editor/Viewer), status (Active/Invited/Deactivated), and last active date. Admins can invite new members, change roles, and deactivate accounts. Has search and role filter at the top.

expectedBehaviors: Tries figma_get_design_system_kit as fallback before telling user to extract
Proceeds with whatever data it gets — does NOT hard-stop
Maps elements to likely components (table, button, input, badge for status)
Plans specific text content ('Invite team member', not 'Action button')
Plans edge cases: empty team (first admin), no search results, deactivation confirmation
antiPatterns: Produces a plan with placeholder text
Ignores the role/permission model in the brief
Doesn't plan the deactivation confirmation flow
Uses generic card grid instead of a table (the brief says table)

evaluationCriteria.degradationGrace: Does it proceed smoothly without design system data?
evaluationCriteria.briefFidelity: Does the plan match EVERY element mentioned in the brief?
evaluationCriteria.edgeCases: Does it plan empty state, no results, and destructive action confirmation?
evaluationCriteria.componentMatching: Does it identify table, button, input, badge as likely components?
